body_txt: Lost in Translation happens quite often because of cultural differences, as it takes a lot of effort to convey complex ideas over the language barrier. But sometimes what is altered is less a grammatical issue as it is the gender of a character. Different mediums can approach this differently, as animation can simply change the voice while a Frankenslation can change the actor . Helpful when a Long-Haired Pretty Boy or Bishōnen looks too girly to pass for a male, or a Tomboy or Lad-ette was made excessively masculine (or even grotesque ) to make the change easier. Reasons this may happen include: Compare Affirmative Action Girl , Hide Your Lesbians , and Getting Crap Past the Radar . In music, it may overlap with The Cover Changes the Gender . Compare Gender Flip and Adaptational Gender Identity when adaptations , rather than translations , change a character's sex or gender. May result in Viewer Gender Confusion , as well as They Changed It, Now It Sucks! . Note that despite the title, this applies to changes in either direction in any language, not just male to female or overseas to Japan. The only possible inversion is if the writer gender-flips a character before the work is released.
